name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language known for its readability and concise syntax. It is used by professionals and beginners worldwide for web development, scientific computing, automation, and more.
usedFor: Web development, data science, machine learning, scripting, automation, desktop applications.
funFact: Python is named after the British comedy group 'Monty Python', not the snake.

terminalCommands.install: Download Python from https://python.org and add it to your PATH. Mac and Linux usually come with Python pre-installed, but you can upgrade via 'brew install python' (Mac) or 'sudo apt install python3' (Ubuntu).

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: Variables store values in a program. In Python, you declare a variable by assigning it a value. There is no need to specify the type explicitly.

keyConcepts.definition: Storing a value in a variable using =
Python determines type automatically based on value.

commonMistakes: Trying to declare variable types as in other languages.
topics.challenge: Create a variable named 'age' set to 25 and print it.
topics.terminalNote: Save as hello.py and run with 'python hello.py'

topics.title: Lists
topics.explanation: Lists store ordered collections of items, which can be of different types. Lists are mutable and use square brackets.

keyConcepts.definition: Content can be changed after creation.
Position of an item, starting at 0.

commonMistakes: IndexError: list index out of range.
topics.challenge: Print the first item in the list: words = ['hi', 'bye']
topics.terminalNote: Save code in hello.py, run in terminal as above.

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich
description: JavaScript is the standard scripting language of the web, running in all major browsers and increasingly on servers via Node.js. It enables interactivity, dynamic content, and responsive UI design.
usedFor: Web development (frontend and backend), mobile app development, desktop applications, automation.
funFact: JavaScript was developed in just 10 days and originally called Mocha.

terminalCommands.install: Download Node.js from https://nodejs.org and install to run JavaScript outside browsers.

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: 'let', 'const', and 'var' are used to declare variables. 'let' is block-scoped, 'const' creates a constant, and 'var' is function-scoped (legacy).
topics.starterCode: // Declare a variable message with 'Hello' and log it
commonMistakes: Trying to reassign a const variable.
topics.challenge: Declare a variable 'count' set to 3 and log it.
topics.hint: Use let or const and console.log().
topics.terminalNote: Save code in hello.js, run with 'node hello.js'

topics.title: Arrays
topics.explanation: Arrays are ordered lists of values. You can access elements by their index and use methods to add, remove, or process elements.

keyConcepts.definition: A list that stores ordered data.

topics.hint: Index starts at 0, second item is index 1.
topics.terminalNote: Save as hello.js and run via node.

name: TypeScript
creator: Anders Hejlsberg
description: TypeScript is a strongly-typed superset of JavaScript that adds static types and modern tools for large-scale software. It compiles to JavaScript and is adopted by major projects to improve reliability.
usedFor: Large-scale web apps, libraries, frameworks (Angular, React, etc.), Node.js backends.
funFact: TypeScript was first announced by Microsoft and shares roots with languages like C#.

terminalCommands.install: Install with 'npm install -g typescript'. Create hello.ts, compile using 'tsc hello.ts'.

topics.title: Type Annotations
topics.explanation: TypeScript allows you to specify types for variables, function parameters, and return values. This lets you catch mistakes before running your code.

keyConcepts.definition: Tells TypeScript what type a variable or parameter should be.

topics.starterCode:
let color
// assign color to a string, and log it
commonMistakes: Forgetting a type or using the wrong type.
Assigning a value of a different type causes compile error.
topics.challenge: Declare a variable animal of type string with value 'dog' and print it.
topics.terminalNote: Save code in hello.ts. Compile with 'tsc hello.ts'. Run 'node hello.js'.

name: C
creator: Dennis Ritchie
description: C is a powerful, low-level programming language that forms the basis for operating systems, databases, and embedded systems. It provides control over memory and hardware.
usedFor: Operating systems (Linux, Windows parts), embedded firmware, databases (MySQL), system utilities.
funFact: The Unix kernel and most of its utilities were originally written in C.

terminalCommands.install: Install GCC. On Windows, use MinGW or WSL. On Mac, install Xcode Command Line Tools. On Ubuntu: sudo apt install build-essential.

topics.title: Variables
topics.explanation: C requires you to declare a variable's type. Types determine the size of memory and what values a variable can store.

keyConcepts.definition: Specifying the type and name of a variable.
Giving your variable a starting value.

commonMistakes: Mismatching types on declaration and assignment.
topics.challenge: Declare an int variable x, set to 12, and print.
topics.terminalNote: Compile with gcc and run as above.

name: C++
creator: Bjarne Stroustrup
description: C++ extends C with object-oriented programming, templates, and powerful standard libraries. It's widely used for high-performance software, games, and system tools.
usedFor: Game development, real-time systems, operating systems, graphics engines, large software.
funFact: C++ originally stood for 'C with Classes'.

terminalCommands.install: Install g++: Windows (MinGW), Mac (Xcode CLI tools), Ubuntu (sudo apt install g++).

topics.title: Classes
topics.explanation: Classes allow you to model real-world objects, grouping data and functions for reuse and organization.

keyConcepts.definition: A function defined inside a class.

commonMistakes: Not using new for dynamic objects when needed.
topics.challenge: Make a class Book with string title, set title and print it.
topics.terminalNote: Save as .cpp, compile and run as above.
